name: Ria Money Transfer - Cardenas
description_short: Ria Money Transfer operates at Cardenas Markets locations across CA, AZ, and NV, offering international money transfers, check cashing, bill payments, and money orders without requiring a bank account.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer is a global remittance service with 35+ years of experience serving over 100 million customers worldwide. At Cardenas Markets, Ria provides in-store financial services to the Latino and immigrant communities across California, Arizona, and Nevada through 65+ retail locations. The partnership allows customers to access money transfer and financial services conveniently while shopping at grocery stores.

Ria's core service at Cardenas Markets is international money transfers to 190+ countries, with particular strength in Latin America (Mexico, El Salvador, Honduras, Guatemala, Nicaragua, Colombia, Peru). Beyond remittances, they offer check cashing without requiring a bank account, bill payments (phone, water, electricity), money orders (up to ten at a time), and mobile top-ups. Customers can send money in-person, online, via mobile app, or through WhatsApp, and recipients can receive funds through bank deposits, cash pickups at 600,000+ worldwide locations, digital wallets, home delivery, or ATM withdrawals.

Ria's competitive advantages at Cardenas Markets include promotional pricing (currently offering 50% off transfer fees with code HCGWB2), extensive destination networks in Latin America with partnerships like BBVA, Banco Azteca, OXXO, and Mercado Pago, and the convenience of in-store access during grocery shopping. The company emphasizes security measures, competitive exchange rates, and multilingual support (English/Spanish). Their 600,000+ global payout locations provide genuine accessibility for recipients.

The primary limitation is geographic availability—services are currently restricted to Heritage Grocers Group locations (Cardenas Markets and Los Altos Ranch Market) in three Western states. While competitive, Ria's rates and fees are not independently verified on their website. The service targets immigrant and international remittance senders rather than domestic consumers, and check cashing is secondary to money transfer services.
address: 235 E Julian St, San Jose, CA 95112

company_info.headquarters: 235 E Julian St, San Jose, CA 95112

pros: No bank account required for check cashing, serving unbanked customers
Promotional 50% fee discount available with promo code HCGWB2
600,000+ payout locations across 190+ countries for recipient access
Multiple send methods: in-person, online, app, WhatsApp, bill payment, and mobile top-up
Strong Latin America network with partnerships (BBVA, Banco Azteca, OXXO, Mercado Pago, DaviPlata)
55,000+ locations in Mexico alone for competitive send options
Multilingual support in English and Spanish; convenient in-store access during grocery shopping
cons: Limited to 65+ Cardenas Markets locations in CA, AZ, NV only—not nationwide
Rates and fees not transparently displayed online; promotional discounts require promo codes
Check cashing is secondary service; primary focus is international money transfers, not domestic financial services
Website does not disclose specific fee structures or exchange rate margins for comparison
Recipient payment methods limited by destination country partnerships and infrastructure
best_for: Unbanked immigrants sending remittances to Latin America from CA, AZ, or NV
Cardenas Markets customers who need check cashing without a bank account
Families sending money to Mexico, El Salvador, Honduras, or Guatemala regularly
Customers seeking in-store convenience combined with international money transfer services
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer at Cardenas Markets is best for immigrant and Latino customers in California, Arizona, and Nevada who lack traditional bank accounts and need to send money internationally—particularly to Latin America—while shopping for groceries. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Heritage Grocers Group locations and lack of transparent fee/rate disclosure online, requiring in-store visits or contact for pricing details.
services: Check cashing without bank account requirement
Money orders (up to ten at a time)
Mobile top-ups (own phone or send credit internationally)
